text: A monk asked Joshu, a Chinese Zen master: "Has a dog Buddha-nature or not?" ✦ "Mu" is the negative symbol in Chinese, meaning "No thing" or "Nay." See also: article on Mu at Wikipedia.
author: Gateless Gate

text: Once when Hyakujo delivered some Zen lectures an old man attended them, unseen by the monks. At the end of each talk when the monks left so did he. But one day he remained after they had gone, and Hyakujo asked him: "Who are you?" ✦ Hyakujo clapped his hands and laughed at this discernment. "I thought a Persian had a red beard," he said, "and now I know a Persian who has a red beard."
author: Gateless Gate

text: Gutei raised his finger whenever he was asked a question about Zen. A boy attendant began to imitate him in this way. When anyone asked the boy what his master had preached about, the boy would raise his finger. ✦ When Gutei was about to pass from this world he gathered his monks around him. "I attained my finger-Zen," he said, "from my teacher Tenryu, and in my whole life I could not exhaust it." Then he passed away.
author: Gateless Gate

text: Wakuan complained when he saw a picture of bearded Bodhidharma: "Why hasn't that fellow a beard?"
author: Gateless Gate

text: Kyogen said: "Zen is like a man hanging in a tree by his teeth over a precipice. His hands grasp no branch, his feet rest on no limb, and under the tree another person asks him: 'Why did Bodhidharma come to China from India?'" ✦ If the man in the tree does not answer, he fails; and if he does answer, he falls and loses his life. Now what shall he do?"
author: Gateless Gate

text: When Buddha was in Grdhrakuta mountain he turned a flower in his fingers and held it before his listeners. Every one was silent. Only Maha-Kashapa smiled at this revelation, although he tried to control the lines of his face. ✦ Buddha said: "I have the eye of the true teaching, the heart of Nirvana, the true aspect of non-form, and the ineffable stride of Dharma. It is not expressed by words, but especially transmitted beyond teaching. This teaching I have given to Maha-Kashapa."
author: Gateless Gate

text: A monk told Joshu: "I have just entered the monastery. Please teach me." ✦ At that moment the monk was enlightened.
author: Gateless Gate

text: Getsuan said to his students: "Keichu, the first wheel-maker of China, made two wheels of fifty spokes each. Now, suppose you removed the nave uniting the spokes. What would become of the wheel? And had Keichu done this, could he be called the master wheel-maker?"
author: Gateless Gate

text: A monk asked Seijo: "I understand that a Buddha who lived before recorded history sat in meditation for ten cycles of existence and could not realize the highest truth, and so could not become fully emancipated. Why was this so?" ✦ Seijo said: "He was not a Buddha."
author: Gateless Gate

text: A monk named Seizei asked of Sozan: "Seizei is alone and poor. Will you give him support?" ✦ Sozan said: "You have Zen, the best wine in China, and already have finished three cups, and still you are saying that they did not even wet your lips."
author: Gateless Gate

text: Joshu went to a place where a monk had retired to meditate and asked him: "What is, is what?" ✦ Joshu said: "Well given, well taken, well killed, well saved." And he bowed to the monk.
author: Gateless Gate
source: Case 11: Joshu Examines a Monk in Meditation

text: Zuigan called out to himself every day: "Master." ✦ "Yes, sir; yes, sir," he answered.
author: Gateless Gate
source: Case 12: Zuigan Calls His Own Master

text: Tokusan went to the dining room from the meditation hall holding his bowl. Seppo was on duty cooking. When he met Tokusan he said: "The dinner drum is not yet beaten. Where are you going with your bowl?" ✦ The next day Tokusan delivered an entirely different kind of lecture to the monks. Ganto laughed and clapped his hands, saying: "I see our old man understands ultimate truth indeed. None in China can surpass him."
author: Gateless Gate

text: Nansen saw the monks of the eastern and western halls fighting over a cat. He seized the cat and told the monks: "If any of you say a good word, you can save the cat." ✦ Nansen said: "If you had been there, you could have saved the cat."
author: Gateless Gate
source: Case 14: Nansen Cuts the Cat in Two

text: sister projects : Wikidata item Koan number 15 out of 49 in this work (published 1228, translated 1934) ✦ Before Ummon's words were ended Tozan was enlightened.
author: Gateless Gate

text: Ummon asked: "The world is such a wide world, why do you answer a bell and don ceremonial robes?"
author: Gateless Gate

text: Chu, called Kokushi, the teacher of the emperor, called to his attendant: "Oshin." ✦ Chu said: "I ought to apologize to you for all this calling, but really you ought to apologize to me."
author: Gateless Gate
source: Case 17: The Three Calls Of The Emperor's Teacher

text: A monk asked Tozan when he was weighing some flax: "What is Buddha?" ✦ Tozan said: "This flax weighs three pounds."
author: Gateless Gate

text: Joshu asked Nansen: "What is the path?" ✦ At these words Joshu was enlightened.
author: Gateless Gate
source: Case 19: Everyday Life Is The Path

text: Shogen asked: "Why does the enlightened man not stand on his feet and explain himself?" And he also said: "It is not necessary for speech to come from the tongue."
author: Gateless Gate

text: A monk asked Ummon: "What is Buddha?" ✦ Ummon answered him: "Dried dung."
author: Gateless Gate

text: Ananda asked Kashapa: "Buddha gave you the golden-woven robe of successorship. What else did he give you?" ✦ Said Kashapa: "Now you can take down my preaching sign and put up your own."
author: Gateless Gate

text: When he became emancipated the sixth patriarch received from the fifth patriarch the bowl and robe given from the Buddha to his successors, generation after generation. ✦ The sixth patriarch replied: "We studied together under the fifth patriarch. Call him your teacher, but just treasure what you have attained."
author: Gateless Gate
source: Case 23: Do Not Think Good, Do Not Think Not-Good

text: A monk asked Fuketsu: "Without speaking, without silence, how can you express the truth?" ✦ Fuketsu observed: "I always remember springtime in southern China. The birds sing among innumerable kinds of fragrant flowers."
author: Gateless Gate

text: In a dream Kyozan went to Maitreya's Pure Land. He recognized himself seated in the third seat in the abode of Maitreya. Someone announced: "Today the one who sits in the third seat will preach." ✦ Kyozan arose and, hitting the gavel, said: "The truth of Mahayana teaching is transcendent, above words and thought. Do you understand?"
author: Gateless Gate
source: Case 25: Preaching From The Third Seat

text: Hogen of Seiryo monastery was about to lecture before dinner when he noticed that the bamboo screen lowered for meditation had not been rolled up. He pointed to it. Two monks arose from the audience and rolled it up. ✦ Hogen, observing the physical moment, said: "The state of the first monk is good, not that of the other."
author: Gateless Gate
source: Case 26: Two Monks Roll Up The Screen

text: A monk asked Nansen: "Is there a teaching no master ever preached before?" ✦ Nansen replied: "It is not mind, it is not Buddha, it is not things."
author: Gateless Gate
source: Case 27: It Is Not Mind, It Is Not Buddha, It Is Not Things

text: Tokusan was studying Zen under Ryutan. One night he came to Ryutan and asked many questions. The teacher said: "The night is getting old. Why don't you retire?"
author: Gateless Gate

text: Two monks were arguing about a flag. One said: "The flag is moving." ✦ The sixth patriarch happened to be passing by. He told them: "Not the wind, not the flag; mind is moving."
author: Gateless Gate
source: Case 29: Not The Wind, Not The Flag

text: Daibai asked Baso: "What is Buddha?" ✦ Baso said: "This mind is Buddha."
author: Gateless Gate

text: A traveling monk asked an old woman the road to Taizan, a popular temple supposed to give wisdom to the one who worships there. The old woman said: "Go straight ahead." When the monk proceeded a few steps, she said to herself: "He also is a common church-goer." ✦ Joshu remarked: "I have investigated that old woman."
author: Gateless Gate

text: A philosopher asked Buddha: "Without words, without the wordless, will you tell me truth?" ✦ The Buddha replied: "A good horse runs even at the shadow of the whip."
author: Gateless Gate

text: A monk asked Baso: "What is Buddha?" ✦ Baso said: "This mind is not Buddha."
author: Gateless Gate
source: Case 33: This Mind Is Not Buddha

text: Nansen said: "Mind is not Buddha. Learning is not the path."
author: Gateless Gate
source: Case 34: Learning Is Not The Path

text: "Seijo, the Chinese girl," observed Goso, "had two souls, one always sick at home and the other in the city, a married woman with two children. Which was the true soul?"
author: Gateless Gate

text: Goso said: "When you meet a Zen master on the road you cannot talk to him, you cannot face him with silence. What are you going to do?"
author: Gateless Gate
source: Case 36: Meeting A Zen Master On The Road

text: Goso said: "When a buffalo goes out of his enclosure to the edge of the abyss, his horns and his head and his hoofs all pass through, but why can't the tail also pass?"
author: Gateless Gate
source: Case 37: A Buffalo Passes Through The Enclosure

text: A monk asked Joshu why Bodhidharma came to China. ✦ Joshu said: "An oak tree in the garden."
author: Gateless Gate
source: Case 38: An Oak Tree In The Garden

text: A Zen student told Ummon: "Brilliancy of Buddha illuminates the whole universe." ✦ Afterwards another teacher, Shishin, asked his pupils: "At what point did that student go off the track?"
author: Gateless Gate

text: Hyakujo wished to send a monk to open a new monastery. He told his pupils that whoever answered a question most ably would be appointed. Placing a water vase on the ground, he asked: "Who can say what this is without calling its name?" ✦ Hyakujo smiled and said: "The chief monk loses." And Isan became the master of the new monastery.
author: Gateless Gate
source: Case 40: Tipping Over A Water Vase

text: Bodhidharma sits facing the wall. His future successor stands in the snow and presents his severed arm to Bodhidharma. He cries: "My mind is not pacified. Master, pacify my mind." ✦ Bodhidharma says: "Then your mind is pacified already."
author: Gateless Gate

text: In the time of Buddha Shakyamuni, Manjusri went to the assemblage of the Buddhas. When he arrived there, the conference was over and each Buddha had returned to his own Buddha-land. Only one girl was yet unmoved in deep meditation. ✦ No sooner had the Buddha spoken than that Bodhisattva sprang up from the earth and bowed and paid homage to the Buddha. Buddha directed him to arouse the girl. The Bodhisattva went in front of the girl and snapped his fingers, and in that instant the girl came out from her deep meditation.
author: Gateless Gate
source: Case 42: The Girl Comes Out From Meditation

text: Shuzan held out his short staff and said: "If you call this a short staff, you oppose its reality. If you do not call it a short staff, you ignore the fact. Now what do you wish to call this?"
author: Gateless Gate

text: Basho said to his disciple: "When you have a staff, I will give it to you. If you have no staff, I will take it away from you."
author: Gateless Gate

text: Sekiso asked: "How can you proceed on from the top of a hundred-foot pole?" Another Zen teacher said: "One who sits on the top of a hundred-foot pole has attained a certain height but still is not handling Zen freely. He should proceed on from there and appear with his whole body in the ten parts of the world."
author: Gateless Gate
source: Case 46: Proceed From The Top Of The Pole

text: Tosotsu built three barriers and made the monks pass through them. The first barrier is studying Zen. In studying Zen the aim is to see one's own true nature. Now where is your true nature? ✦ Thirdly, if you free yourself from birth and death, you should know where you are. Now your body separates into the four elements. Where are you?
author: Gateless Gate

text: A Zen pupil asked Kembo: "All Buddhas of the ten parts of the universe enter the one road of Nirvana. Where does that road begin?" ✦ This pupil went to Ummon and asked the same question. Ummon, who happened to have a fan in his hand, said: "This fan will reach to the thirty-third heaven and hit the nose of the presiding deity there. It is like the Dragon Carp of the Eastern Sea tipping over the rain-cloud with his tail."
author: Gateless Gate

text: Amban, a layman Zen student, said: "Mu-mon has just published forty-eight koans and called the book Gateless Gate. He criticizes the old patriarchs’ words and actions. I think he is very mischievous. He is like an old doughnut seller trying to catch a passerby to force his doughnuts down his mouth. The customer can neither swallow nor spit out the doughnuts, and this causes suffering. Mu-mon has annoyed everyone enough, so I think I shall add one more as a bargain. I wonder if he himself can eat this bargain. If he can, and digest it well, it will be fine, but if not, we will have to put it back into the frying pan with his forty-eight also and cook them again. Mu-mon, you eat first, before someone else does: ✦ "Buddha, according to a sutra, once said: 'Stop, stop. Do not speak. The ultimate truth is not even to think.'"
author: Gateless Gate
